videos.title: 🫚 How to Juice Ginger & Turmeric | Kuvings AUTO10 Plus Juicer

transcript.fullText: Tips for Juicing Ginger & Turmeric Ginger and turmeric are commonly juiced in small amounts as part of a recipe and typically pose no issues. But, if you want to juice ginger and turmeric in larger amounts or on their own, you may run into issues with clogging. 1. Ginger Ginger and turmeric are fibrous ingredients with ginger being more fibrous than turmeric. A buildup of their long stringy fibers can cause clogging. The key is to chop ginger in the opposite direction of its fibers to break them up. We recommend cutting ginger into small chunks, about 1-2 inches. Juicing chopped ginger is quicker, more efficient, and puts less strain on your juicer’s motor. While juicing ginger, you may notice that juice produces quickly and little pulp exits the spout. This is normal. Ginger is high in water content and light in weight, so there's not much pressure pushing ginger pulp through the juicer. 2. Turmeric Turmeric is smaller and less fibrous than ginger, so it doesn’t need to be chopped. But, it needs to be juiced with lemon so your juicer doesn’t become heavily stained from the curcumin in turmeric. You should have no issues juicing whole turmeric. But in case you do, you can chop it using the same method as ginger. As you saw, we juiced both ginger and turmeric with their peels intact. You don’t need to peel them because their peels are thin and don’t cause clogging. If you'd rather peel your ginger, using a spoon is a great option as it can reach into the crevices and remove the skin without wasting the inner flesh. Turmeric is a bit easier to peel, so it can be peeled normally with a peeler. Just be mindful of turmeric staining your hands. If you’re juicing a lot of ginger and turmeric with other ingredients, layer them at the bottom of the hopper and place hard ingredients on top. The hard ingredients will help push the fibers, leading to smooth and easy juicing. 3. Cleaning After juicing any ingredient or recipe, you may notice some residue left in the strainer and screw. This is normal because there is no pressure from other ingredients to push the final pieces out of the juicer. We recommend cleaning your juicer right away and soaking the parts in water, dish soap, and baking soda to remove the stains. Rub some of the paste that forms on the stains and let them sit for a few hours or overnight. For the screw, you can apply a little bit of vegetable oil to a paper towel and rub it onto the stained parts. We hope our tips worked and that you have a smoother juicing experience with ginger and turmeric. Thanks for watching!

segments.text: Tips for Juicing Ginger & Turmeric
Ginger and turmeric are commonly juiced in small amounts as part of a recipe
and typically pose no issues.
But, if you want to juice ginger and turmeric in larger amounts or on their own,
you may run into issues with clogging.
1. Ginger
Ginger and turmeric are fibrous ingredients with ginger being more fibrous than turmeric.
A buildup of their long stringy fibers can cause clogging.
The key is to chop ginger in the opposite direction of its fibers to break them up.
We recommend cutting ginger into small chunks, about 1-2 inches.
Juicing chopped ginger is quicker, more efficient,
and puts less strain on your juicer’s motor.
While juicing ginger, you may notice that juice produces quickly
and little pulp exits the spout.
This is normal.
Ginger is high in water content and light in weight,
so there's not much pressure pushing ginger pulp through the juicer.
2. Turmeric
Turmeric is smaller and less fibrous than ginger,
so it doesn’t need to be chopped.
But, it needs to be juiced with lemon
so your juicer doesn’t become heavily stained from the curcumin in turmeric.
You should have no issues juicing whole turmeric.
But in case you do, you can chop it using the same method as ginger.
As you saw, we juiced both ginger and turmeric with their peels intact.
You don’t need to peel them because their peels are thin and don’t cause clogging.
If you'd rather peel your ginger, using a spoon is a great option
as it can reach into the crevices and remove the skin without wasting the inner flesh.
Turmeric is a bit easier to peel, so it can be peeled normally with a peeler.
Just be mindful of turmeric staining your hands.
If you’re juicing a lot of ginger and turmeric with other ingredients,
layer them at the bottom of the hopper and place hard ingredients on top.
The hard ingredients will help push the fibers,
leading to smooth and easy juicing.
3. Cleaning
After juicing any ingredient or recipe,
you may notice some residue left in the strainer and screw.
This is normal because there is no pressure from other ingredients to push the final pieces out of the juicer.
We recommend cleaning your juicer right away
and soaking the parts in water, dish soap, and baking soda to remove the stains.
Rub some of the paste that forms on the stains
and let them sit for a few hours or overnight.
For the screw, you can apply a little bit of vegetable oil to a paper towel
and rub it onto the stained parts.
We hope our tips worked and that you have a smoother juicing experience with ginger and turmeric.
Thanks for watching!

videos.title: 13 Amazing Health Benefits of Turmeric and Ginger Together

transcript.fullText: turmeric and ginger are two of the most potent herbs on the planet when combined they create a powerful synergy that provides a wealth of health benefits watch this video to learn more about the health benefits of these two spices and find out how you can add them to your diet here are the health benefits of turmeric and ginger mixture one turmeric and ginger help improve digestion turmeric has been used for centuries in ayurvedic medicine to treat various digestive problems it is also known as the king of spices because of its powerful healing properties and flavor turmeric is one of the most potent spices on earth and contains over 600 compounds including curcuminoids responsible for its bright yellow color and healing properties curcuminoids have been found to reduce inflammation in the body and help improve digestion when combined with ginger turmeric is an effective remedy for digestive disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis ginger helps your body break down food as it aids in the breakdown of proteins into amino acids 2. turmeric and ginger mixture enhances brain functions this mixture is one of the best ways to improve your overall brain health it improves memory concentration focus and mood it also supports brain functions that are related to learning and mental acuity it's possible thanks to compounds that are present in turmeric these compounds include curcumin which aids in absorbing certain nutrients and improves brain function ginger contains compounds such as gingerol and chobal which can also be very helpful in improving brain function people with alzheimer's disease have a weakened immune system that allows the formation of proteins that clump together in the brain this mixture of proteins forms clumps that block the brain's blood supply the compounds in turmeric and ginger are potent antioxidants meaning that they can slow the growth of these harmful molecules called free radicals free radicals can damage dna proteins and other molecules these compounds help get rid of free radicals and restore the blood flow to the brain you can never go wrong with a good spice combo 3. turmeric and ginger help relieve inflammation both turmeric and ginger contain powerful antioxidants that protect cells from damage caused by free radicals compounds such as curcumin an active ingredient in turmeric are thought to help reduce inflammation ginger contains compounds such as gingerol and chogel which can also be very helpful in relieving inflammation 4. turmeric and ginger are great for the immune system turmeric and ginger contain compounds that support the immune system by helping to produce white blood cells these spices contain compounds that help to boost the body's ability to fight infection the compounds include curcumin and gingerol which play a key role in fighting viruses they also help stimulate the body to produce antibodies proteins that recognize and destroy bacteria and other fungi 5. turmeric and ginger help fight cancer curcumin a compound in turmeric has been shown to kill colon cancer cells in the laboratory without harming healthy cells research suggests that curcumin may help inhibit the development of colon cancer and may slow its progression by preventing the growth of new blood vessels that feed the tumor other research has shown that ginger appears to have a similar effect on cells in different types of cancer such as breast cancer 6. turmeric and ginger may improve heart health the compounds in turmeric and ginger are powerful antioxidants meaning that they can slow the growth of harmful molecules called free radicals free radicals can damage dna proteins and other molecules the mixture gets rid of the free radicals and prevents them from damaging the body's cells these spices feature compounds that reduce the effects of plaque buildup in the arteries leading to lower rates of heart attacks and strokes the mixture has been shown to have a beneficial impact on the health of our hearts helping to lower ldl cholesterol which can lead to cardiovascular disease curcuminoids in turmeric may also help to reduce blood pressure levels seven turmeric and ginger may be suitable for your skin a compound in turmeric called curcumin has been found to have anti-inflammatory effects that help improve the way your skin heals after a wound or other injury ginger has also been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and scientists are currently researching ways to use it to treat skin conditions including acne and eczema eight turmeric and ginger reduces pain when you consume turmeric or ginger they have a soothing effect on the body reducing pain and inflammation caused by arthritis fibromyalgia and other painful conditions it's possible thanks to the compounds in turmeric and ginger called curcuminoids that have anti-inflammatory properties turmeric is also a potent antioxidant which helps to protect your body from damage caused by free radicals 9. turmeric and ginger can help you lose weight ginger has anti-inflammatory properties which reduce inflammation in the body making it easier to burn fat and lose weight additionally in ayurvedic medicine turmeric is known as rasayana for thousands of years in india it has been used to reduce weight and improve overall health 10. turmeric and ginger can protect you from the flu ginger and turmeric can help to keep you healthy during the flu season studies have found that people who eat ginger are less likely to get sick with the flu or cold both ginger and turmeric contain active ingredients that are thought to kill the flu virus this property is why they're used in many cold remedies sold at health food stores 11. turmeric and ginger can help to lower your blood pressure the active ingredient in turmeric curcumin has been found to reduce blood pressure in rats and it has also been shown to have the same effect in humans who take it for a short period turmeric may help lower blood pressure by increasing nitric oxide levels in the body nitric oxide is a potent constrictor of blood vessels which helps to control blood pressure 12. turmeric and ginger can help to improve blood circulation turmeric and ginger also positively affect the body's blood circulation they have been shown to improve blood circulation in rats and humans people with low blood circulation have a higher risk of developing heart diseases including heart failure and strokes these conditions are often caused by atherosclerosis hardening of the arteries and high blood pressure blood circulation improves with the help of turmeric and ginger 13. turmeric and ginger can help to lower your cholesterol levels the active ingredient in turmeric curcumin has reduced cholesterol levels in mice in a study published in the journal of food science researchers found that curcumin induced the breakdown of low density lipoprotein ldl cholesterol and mice some of the compounds featured in ginger also possess this attribute moderate cholesterol levels can reduce the risk of heart disease and stroke as you can see turmeric has a long history of use in ayurvedic medicine it has been used to treat various conditions including joint pain wounds cancer and heart disease it has also been used as a culinary and medicinal spice for thousands of years on the other hand ginger is a rhizome grown for its roots the latest research shows that the root can help you get more energy and support your overall health a combination of both spices when used in a daily diet can enhance your immune system help you relax improve your digestion and your overall health i hope you have enjoyed this video if you have please subscribe to this youtube channel and don't forget to give this video the thumbs up and i'm sure you're going to enjoy this next video on 58 amazing health benefits uses and beauty tips of turmeric thanks for watching and bye for now

videos.title: It Tastes Too Good to be This Healthy! ~ Golden/Turmeric Milk Recipe

transcript.fullText: ever since I've learned about Golden milk I haven't been able to stop drinking it you know I ordered one of these in a cafe not long ago and it cost me $8 I couldn't believe it yet I made this one at home for less than $1 and you can too and I can promise you this guys this one's going to taste way better than that one you'll get in the cafe in case you didn't know golden milk is also known as turmeric milk and it's full of powerful antioxidants that's perfect for this time of year it helps you sleep at night if you have insomnia but heck I just drink it cuz it tastes so dang good so let's make ourselves a cup of golden milk to a small pot I'm going to add one nice big mug full of milk I'm using oat milk but you can use any milk of your choice now I'm going to add half a teaspoon of turmeric and basically it's half a teaspoon of turmeric per serving now I'm going to add half a teaspoon of fresh ginger and another half a teaspoon of fresh ginger because what can I say guys I love Ginger now I'm going to add about an eighth of a teaspoon of black pepper and as you can see I'm just eyeballing it and I'm also going to add about an eighth of a teaspoon of cinnamon now don't overdo it on the cinnamon or your milk is going to turn brown the turmeric is the star guys now I'm going to whisk this together and put the heat on high and you don't want to walk away from this or it will boil over and just a quick reminder if you're looking for a printable version of this recipe I have one up on the blog the link will be down in the description and if you're enjoying this video let me know by hitting that like button it helps out the channel so much and I really appreciate it now as soon as it starts to boil turn it down to medium low and bring it to a gentle simmer now we're going to let it cook gently like this for about 3 to 5 minutes just enough time to extract all that goodness from the spices and the ginger and give it a little mix now and then just to be sure it's not burning on the bottom that can happen depending on what type of milk you use now if you want a nice foam on your golden milk like you see on a latte and say you don't have one of those fancy frothers just whisk it really vigorously you'll see that the more you do this the the foamier it's going to get and that's going to give you that nice creamy latte mouth feel now I'm going to let this cool for several minutes before adding my last ingredient which is honey good old honey and I'm using raw unpasturized honey and if I were to add this into the piping hot golden milk well the heat would pasteurize the honey and we wouldn't get any of its health benefits so do be sure to let your golden milk cool for several minutes until it's just nice and warm not super hot so I'm going to add a nice spoonful of that and of of course you could also use agave syrup maple syrup you could even use plain sugar if you want now just whisk that in until it's nice and smooth now before we serve this up we have to remember there's still pieces of Ginger floating in there so we have to pass this through a SE that's going to leave all those little ginger pieces behind and you'll have a nice smooth creamy golden milk wow look at how pretty that looks and it's basically a must to finish this off with a nice little sprinkling of cinnamon so I'm just going to put some on there and wow I feel like such an Instagram star just look at how gorgeous that beverage is and when you try it I think you're going to agree that golden milk tastes as intriguing as it sounds the flavors are warm and spicy and sweet and it's hard to believe that something this tasty is that good for you but it is and well you just got to love it when that happens

videos.title: The Incredible Benefits of Carrot Juice & Turmeric

transcript.fullText: carrot juice and turmeric together they're more than just a pretty golden drink this combination packs a scientifically proven punch offering incredible benefits for your skin immunity and overall health let's dive into the fascinating research behind this Powerhouse Duo carrots have been a health staple for centuries but carrot juice takes it to the next level it's loaded with beta carotene a precursor to vitamin A which plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy skin boosting your immune system and protecting your vision according to a study published in the journal nutrition research consuming beta katene rich foods can significantly improve Skin's natural defenses against UV damage while promoting collagen production for smoother skin and then there's turmeric the golden spice of ayurveda and traditional medicine its active compound kirkin has been extensively studied for its powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects in a review published in advances in experimental medicine and biology kkin was shown to reduce markers of inflammation and oxidative stress which are linked to conditions like arthritis heart disease and even cognitive decline but here's the thing kirkin isn't easily absorbed by the body that's where carrot juice steps in when combined carrot juice and turmeric create a synergistic effect the natural fats and carrot juice enhance kirkman's bioavailability allowing your body to absorb and use it effectively research published in the journal molecules highlights how circumin absorption increases dramatically when consumed with healthy fats or compounds like piperine found in black pepper adding a pinch of black pepper to your turmeric and carrot juice can make the drink even more potent let's break down what this combination can do for your body backed by science one radiant skin the antioxidants in carrot juice and curcumin neutralize free radicals reducing skin damage and promoting a youthful glow studies in dermatoendocrinology show that carotenoids and carrots also improve skin tone and texture two stronger immunity vitamin A in carrot juice enhances the production of white blood cells while turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help your immune system respond better to threats three reduced inflammation chronic inflammation is linked to diseases like arthritis heart disease and diabetes a 2016 study in the Journal of medicinal food confirmed turmeric's ability to reduce inflammatory markers especially when paired with bioavailability enhancers like carrot juice fat four improve digestion turmeric stimulates bof flow aiding fat digestion while carrot juice soothes the gut lining promoting better overall Digestive Health five natural detoxification both ingredients support liver function carrot juice helps with toxin elimination and curcumin protects liver cells from oxidative damage ready to make this at home here's a simple recipe five fresh carrots for a base of beta keratin add 1/4 taspo of turmeric powder or a small piece of fresh turmeric root for enhanced absorption sprinkle a pinch of black pepper optional add a squeeze of lemon for a zesty vitamin C boost mix well pour into your favorite glass and enjoy people who drink this combination regularly report feeling more energetic having clearer skin and experiencing less joint pain and it's no surprise science backs these benefits so why not give this golden drink a try your body mind and skin will thank you if you enjoyed this video hit like subscribe and share it with someone who loves Natural Health remedies and check the description below for links to the research studies mentioned [Music] [Music]

videos.title: Turmeric Shots Recipe - How To Make Turmeric Shots - Boosts Immunity Overall Health | Skinny Recipes

transcript.fullText: hey guys i'm nisha homie and today i'm sharing turmeric shorts recipe an easy way to incorporate the goodness of turmeric in your diet as well as your family's diet turmeric shorts are easy to make and can be enjoyed by the whole family to help boost immunity and overall health so let's get started with the recipe to make turmeric shots into a saucepan i am adding in one cup of water turn on the flame and warm the water that is you just need to lightly heat the water once the water is warm or is lightly heated reduce the flame to the lowest pour 3 4 of the warmed water into a cup and now only 1 4 cup of water is left on the saucepan place the saucepan onto the stove top again put the flame on high and bring this to a boil and once the water starts to boil reduce the flame to the lowest add in 1 teaspoon organic turmeric powder today i'm using bygone turmeric which has high curcumin content two pinches of black pepper powder black pepper helps in better assimilation of curcumin which is the active ingredient in turmeric absorb faster into your body so try not to skip it now give this a good stir let it steep on low flame for about 30 to 40 seconds after 30 to 40 seconds turn off the flame immediately pour the turmeric mix into the cup make sure that the water is warm and is not very hot once you made sure that the water is warm you can add in honey as a sweetener now when you're using honey make sure that you are using a good organic honey add honey as per your taste i am adding in two teaspoons of the organic honey when you are adding honey always make sure that the water is lukewarm that is it is just warm and it is not very hot honey should never be added to very hot or very cold food give this a good mix for better absorption of the active ingredient in turmeric known as curcumin you can add in a little bit of healthy fats i am using wood pressed coconut oil and i used about 1 4 teaspoon of wood pressed coconut oil instead of wood pressed coconut oil you can use a to desi ghee or any wood pressed or cold pressed oil of your choice if you're making this for children you can increase the quantity of honey to suit their taste you can also have this shorts without honey but if you would like to sweeten it honey is a good option instead of honey you can use date paste or jaggery liquid jaggery maple syrup any sweetener of your choice is ideal pour into short glasses and enjoy the turmeric shots with your family you can have it as an immune boosting drink any time of the day so guys do try this healthy turmeric shots recipe and let me know how it turned out thank you for watching and until next time take care bye

videos.title: How to Make Fresh Ginger Turmeric Juice Shots the Cheap WAY!

transcript.fullText:
So the other day I had a question on
Facebook about making turmeric ginger juice shots. And first off I normally
don't really like to juice things because there's a great company down the
road called Grounding Roots and on the occasions that I do want to have
the occasional occasional green juice or their popsicles--I really like those--a
lot of times I mean sugar even if it's from a natural source is still sugar and
I try to keep things pretty little carb. I just work best that way. So I don't
really juice too often. But I really do like that whole ginger tumeric juice
thing and I found... this is the box but I have the red one I knew that I was going to
be using this to juice ginger and turmeric. Turmeric will probably dye my
fingers nice and yellow. So I chose this darker colored juicer just because
I knew what would happen and no matter what it will stain and this one is it's
stained even the clear parts. Anyway this is about $20 on Amazon which if you've
priced out other masticating juicers which is what you need to juice roots
like ginger and turmeric which I'm going to show you the tumeric in a little bit
this is ginger this should be very familiar. The turmeric
might be new for some of you guys normally we see it powdered this is a
little bit of a different animal. But anyway, so $20 on Amazon I'll put a link
below for you and it's a great value. You can also use it for wheatgrass. So for
someone like myself who enjoys the occasional fresh wheatgrass shot or
ginger turmeric shot at home this little $20 juicer is just perfect. Would I want
to use it every day? No. Do I want to have these juice shots every day?
Absolutely no. I usually have them if I'm feeling just
kind of ehh or if there's the slightest hint of a cold coming on or if I just
feel like doing it which is the case tonight. And I'll usually make a joke
about it being Saturday night we're doing shots... of ginger-turmeric juice! Anyway... off we go.
In my answer to my friend online I said use about two ounces of each root.
And you can measure those I kind of eyeball it now but if you want to start
by using a food scale that's a recommended method to kind of figure out
how much you like to use of either. You also now might want to eyeball
it but then it'll weigh them along the way and figure out what it is you like. I
don't get that scientific with it it's not an exact science. You may have your
own preference with these. So, what I like to put in is ginger that's obvious,
turmeric and let me show you these. If you've never seen a fresh turmeric root
they look a little interesting. I chose larger ones on purpose so they would be
easier to show to you and also easier in general to handle. These
are turmeric roots. Can you see those? It's kind of hard to hold them all up at
once. So they look a little bit like the ginger they have some of the same

transcript.fullText: These bottles might look simple, but they are loaded with power. Hey faves, welcome back to my kitchen. Let me show you how I made this from scratch. Here is everything I used. Pineapple to sweeten and aid digestion. Oranges for juice and balance. Lemon for vitamin C. Ginger for that kick and inflammation support. Turmeric for healing and to help boost immunity. I started by peeling and cutting my pineapples into tiny chunks. I did this because we're going to using a blender. You don't want to have them in large chunks. You can absolutely skip this step if you're using a juicer. We're doing this because we're using a blender. We also did the same for the lemon and the oranges. You want to keep everything a manageable sizes. That way it's easier on the blender. [Music] It does not have to be perfect. Just clean, fresh, and ready to blend. Ginger and turmeric can be a bit rough to handle. I didn't scrape off the skin of my turmeric and ginger, but you can scrape off yours if that's what you're comfortable with. I just went ahead and cut this into tiny manable chunks. No need for perfect cut. Just keep everything simple and clean. Everything goes into the blender and some water. Remember not to add too much water. For this particular quantity, I use two glasses of water. Go ahead and blend this. Blend until it is very smooth. This next part is where the real work happens. I strain using a mesh cloth to separate the juice from the P. It takes a bit of time and effort, but once you see that coding liquid dropping, you just know it's what every bit of your time. Go ahead and squeeze this very well to get out every bit of juice. Here we have our freshly made ginger turmeric juice. Turmeric stains a lot, so you can go ahead and use a glove if you're not comfortable with having your fingers looking like mine. I p this into two bottles and store them in my fridge. It stays fresh for a few days and it gives a good boost. I take one shot every morning on an empty stomach. And that is it. Simple and effective. No special equipment needed. If this was helpful, like this video, share it with someone, and subscribe for more real kitchen content like this. Thank you for watching.

videos.title: 8 ANTI-INFLAMMATORY DRINKS | to enjoy for health & wellness

transcript.fullText:
- Last year I did a video
on anti inflammatory foods that I eat every week and with over two and a
half million views now, I'd say it's been quite popular. So today I thought I
would follow that video up with one on anti inflammatory
drinks that I enjoy drinking and I think you will too. These drinks deliver a variety
of nutrients, antioxidants and free radical fighting compounds to keep inflammation at bay and give our immune
systems a little boost. And I think that we could all use that, especially this year. I've got a vibrant
assortment of drinks today, but you'll certainly notice
several orange colored drinks and that's because turmeric is the star ingredient in several of them from warm teas to cooling smoothies. Now before someone mentions
that I forgot golden milk in today's video, I've purposefully left it off because I've already done
a separate video on it and I thought I would give you
some new fresh recipes today but it is absolutely a classic
anti inflammatory drink. One last thing and that's a quick reminder that you can find the printable versions of all of these recipes and
more over on my website. So without further ado, let's get started. The first drink we're gonna make is this deep and dark elderberry tea. You've likely heard of elderberry before because it's the immune boosting fruit that's used in many over the
counter cold and flu treatments like lozenges and syrups. But today we'll avoid any
added sugars, colorings and other ingredients with a
simple three ingredient tea and get those health benefits
directly from the source. So add two cups of water to a pot along with two tablespoons
of dried elderberries. And you'll notice the berries instantly start releasing
their inky color. Then add one cinnamon stick which not only infuses a lovely flavor but is also anti inflammatory. Bring the pot to a boil
then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 15 minutes. Once the tea has finished
simmering, turn off the heat and let it rest for five
minutes then strain it. I usually strain it into a
larger measuring cup first because I'm prone to spilling then I'll pour it into a cup. This quick and easy tea
is warming on a cold day but if it's hot where you live, just chill this tea once you've
made it and pour it over ice for a refreshing iced tea option. (gentle music) Next on our list is a
drink I learned how to make when I spent two months in Indonesia and that's jamu. Unfortunately, I was
accidentally glutened in Bali and had a horrible autoimmune flare and this traditional jamu
drink was definitely my savior. While there are variations to jamu, the key ingredient is usually turmeric and for this I do recommend
purchasing fresh turmeric root if you can get it. Turmeric root is similar in
size and shape to ginger root but if you break it open,
it's a vibrant orange color whereas ginger is a light yellow color. And both of these together make for one powerhouse
anti inflammatory drink. To make it you'll need approximately one cup of roughly chopped turmeric and one finger sized piece of ginger. Because we're going to
strain this at the end you can actually skip the step of peeling if you prefer not to stain your fingers, just give your turmeric
and ginger a good scrub. I fluctuate and sometimes I
peel it and sometimes I don't. Slice it into pieces about
a quarter inch thick or so and measure that out and
then add it to your blender. To that sprinkle a little black pepper as black pepper can help
to increase the absorption of curcumin from the turmeric. Pour in four cups of coconut water which has antioxidants and electrolytes and then blend it up on high
speed for about a minute or until it's fairly smooth. (gentle music) Pour that into a pot
and bring it to a boil then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 20 minutes. After it's done simmering,
turn off the heat and add the juice from one lemon and two tablespoons of
honey and give it a stir. Strain the mixture through
a fine mesh strainer or a nut milk bag, but just be aware that it
will be definitely stain your nut milk bag along with
your clothes, countertop and anything else that
comes into contact with so do be careful. Again I strain it into a
much larger measuring bowl with a pour spout for less spills and then pour that into a glass jar that I keep in the fridge. You can enjoy jamu, chilled or warm and it will last for about
a week in the fridge, just give it a good shake before serving. And I'll usually enjoy about
a half a cup at a time. (gentle music) For the next drink, it's sort of a combination
of the first two because it's a turmeric tea
and it's really easy to make. Just add two cups of water to a pot and this time we'll use ground turmeric rather than fresh turmeric
just to provide some options. So add a half a teaspoon
of ground turmeric along with a quarter
teaspoon of black pepper and two tablespoons of lemon juice, which is about the amount
you'll get from one lemon. Bring that to a boil
and once it's boiling, turn the heat down to low
and simmer for 10 minutes. Once the tea has finished
simmering, turn off the heat, add a little honey if
you'd like, give it a stir and let the tea cool for a minute or two. Pour the tea into a mug through a strainer to remove the black pepper and again try not to spill
because of the turmeric. If you do have any spills just make sure to wipe them up right away. For a little extra lemon flair, I always like to add a
few fresh lemon slices. And I will say that
this tea while delicious was still piping hot so
I took a very little sip. You guys have been asking
for this next recipe for the last year and that's ginger shots. Store bought ginger
shots are crazy expensive and this tastes exactly like
core brand wellness shots. So now you can make them at home. For this recipe you'll need a quarter cup of roughly
chopped ginger root and again you can peel
or not peel the ginger. You'll also need a
third cup of lemon juice which is about three to four
lemons depending on their size. Add the ginger and lemon juice
to a high powered blender along with a quarter cup of coconut water and a pinch or a little
sprinkle of cayenne pepper. Then add the lid, blend for
about a minute or so on high or until the mixture is smooth. (gentle music) Once it's blended, strain the liquid through
a fine mesh strainer. You also wanna press on the
solids with the back of a spoon to extract as much juice as possible. Pour the strain liquid into a shot glass and drink all at once. And yes, these are just as
intensely flavored and spicy as the store bought ones. (gentle music) Next on our list is a cool and refreshing pineapple turmeric smoothie, that's perfect for when
the weather is hot outside. For this recipe I'll grate about a tablespoon of fresh turmeric and one teaspoon of fresh ginger. And for this drink because we're
not straining it at the end you do wanna peal both
the turmeric and ginger. In your high powered blender
add one cup of dairy free milk, two cups of frozen pineapple chunks which are loaded with
enzymes and antioxidants, one banana that's high in potassium and will help to make this smoothie creamy and the grated turmeric and ginger. Then blend that until
it's nice and smooth. (gentle music) If you want a mild turmeric drink with a sweet tropical flair that will have you feeling
good from the inside out this is the drink for you. For an anti inflammatory smoothie that doesn't include turmeric, but it's still loaded with
nutrients and antioxidants, I've got this luscious blueberry smoothie. Add two cups of coconut
water to your blender along with two cups of frozen blueberries, one frozen banana, half
a cup of yogurt or kefir for beneficial probiotics and you can use dairy free as well. And one tablespoon of flax seeds which are high in healthy omega three fats and will help to thicken the smoothie. Then add the lid, blend that together for about 30 seconds or so or until it's beautifully
creamy and beautifully purple. (gentle music) Pour the smoothie into a glass, pop in a straw if you'd like and sip on this deliciously
sweet and power-packed drink. You know I love green smoothies and I have several recipes on my website but today I wanted to show
you how to make green juice and this is a copycat of
one that I used to buy at Pressed Juicery. To make this you'll need two
tablespoons of lime juice which will usually get
from one juice lime, one cucumber that sliced and
we'll add some natural liquid to our green juice, three stalks of celery that
you've roughly chopped, and one apple that you've
cored and sliced into pieces and you can use any variety
of apple that you'd like. Pour the lime juice into a blender along with the cucumber, apple, a small chunk of fresh ginger,
celery, three kale leaves and you can just tear
the leaves from the stems and one cup of spinach. Your blender will be
pretty full at this point and because we're not adding
any water to this recipe, you'll need to use your
tamper to push the ingredients into the blades. And to make things easier on your blender make sure to add the ingredients
that contain more water like the cucumber and
apple to the blender first. But after a minute or so
this should blend on its own just like any other green smoothie. (gentle music) Of course you can drink
it thick and full of fiber but I know some people have
a hard time digesting fiber so to turn this into a green juice, pour it into a nut milk bag
and bring it out over a bowl, then pour it into a glass. This is one nutrient packed green juice with a hint of natural
sweetness from the apple. And if you've ever wondered
how to make green juice without a juicer, well
this is how you do it. The last anti inflammatory
drink on our list today is this apple carrot beet smoothie. And you can tell it's
brimming with antioxidants from its vibrant red color. I always say this smoothie is the equivalent of drinking your veggies and it starts with three carrots
that you've peeled, trimmed and cut into two inch chunks. You'll also add one medium beet, and to peel or not peel the
carrot and beet is up to you. I tend to do just a rough
peel on both of these and then slice the beet into large chunks. For natural sweetness
core slice one apple, whichever variety you'd like and then juice one large orange. Add the orange juice to
your high powered blender along with one cup of water, the sliced apple, carrots, beet and a small chunk of fresh ginger. Add the lid, blend it on
high for about a minute or until the veggies are
broken down and smooth. (gentle music) There's so many vitamins and nutrients loaded in this smoothie
and it's nice and thick. But now that you know how to
make juice from a smoothie, you could always strain this
one as well if you'd like. We all have different tastes
and texture preferences and I am all about giving you options. (gentle music) I hope you guys enjoyed
all of those drink recipes and as a reminder, moderation
and variety are key. So have fun switching up all
of the different healthy foods and drinks that you enjoy weekly. If you like this video, let me
know by giving it a thumbs up and feel free to share
in the comments below your favorite anti inflammatory drinks. Bye, that's it for me this week. I will see you again in the next video. (gentle music)

just to provide some options.

So add a half a teaspoon
of ground turmeric

along with a quarter
teaspoon of black pepper
and two tablespoons of lemon juice,

which is about the amount
you'll get from one lemon.

Bring that to a boil
and once it's boiling,

turn the heat down to low
and simmer for 10 minutes.

Once the tea has finished
simmering, turn off the heat,

add a little honey if
you'd like, give it a stir
and let the tea cool for a minute or two.
Pour the tea into a mug through a strainer
to remove the black pepper

and again try not to spill
because of the turmeric.
If you do have any spills
just make sure to wipe them up right away.
For a little extra lemon flair,

I always like to add a
few fresh lemon slices.

And I will say that
this tea while delicious

was still piping hot so
I took a very little sip.

You guys have been asking
for this next recipe
for the last year and that's ginger shots.

Store bought ginger
shots are crazy expensive

and this tastes exactly like
core brand wellness shots.
So now you can make them at home.
For this recipe you'll need

a quarter cup of roughly
chopped ginger root

and again you can peel
or not peel the ginger.

You'll also need a
third cup of lemon juice

which is about three to four
lemons depending on their size.

Add the ginger and lemon juice
to a high powered blender
along with a quarter cup of coconut water

and a pinch or a little
sprinkle of cayenne pepper.

Then add the lid, blend for
about a minute or so on high
or until the mixture is smooth.
(gentle music)
Once it's blended,

strain the liquid through
a fine mesh strainer.

You also wanna press on the
solids with the back of a spoon
to extract as much juice as possible.
Pour the strain liquid into a shot glass
and drink all at once.

And yes, these are just as
intensely flavored and spicy
as the store bought ones.
(gentle music)
Next on our list is a cool and refreshing
pineapple turmeric smoothie,

that's perfect for when
the weather is hot outside.
For this recipe I'll grate
about a tablespoon of fresh turmeric
and one teaspoon of fresh ginger.

And for this drink because we're
not straining it at the end

you do wanna peal both
the turmeric and ginger.

In your high powered blender
add one cup of dairy free milk,
two cups of frozen pineapple chunks

which are loaded with
enzymes and antioxidants,
one banana that's high in potassium
and will help to make this smoothie creamy
and the grated turmeric and ginger.

Then blend that until
it's nice and smooth.
(gentle music)
If you want a mild turmeric drink
with a sweet tropical flair

that will have you feeling
good from the inside out
this is the drink for you.
For an anti inflammatory smoothie
that doesn't include turmeric,

but it's still loaded with
nutrients and antioxidants,
I've got this luscious blueberry smoothie.

Add two cups of coconut
water to your blender
along with two cups of frozen blueberries,

one frozen banana, half
a cup of yogurt or kefir

turmeric root or half teaspoon turmeric powder handful of fresh coriander leaves 1 cup water one teaspoon of honey for sweetness if required now let's see how to prepare this juice wash all the transcript.fullText: the beautiful bright color of beetroot can make anyone fall in love with them yet they're ignored perhaps because of their distinctive taste if you're not a fan you might want to think of an easier way to still enjoy the many health benefits of beetroot without having to actually eat it meat roots are best enjoyed raw and uncooked and juicing them up is a quick and simple method of consuming them add ginger to the mix which is known for being one of the most powerful natural medicines in the world and you're on to a winner here's everything you need to know about the health benefits of beetroot and ginger juice one it manages your blood pressure one of the benefits of beetroot juice and ginger is that it can help in temporarily lowering high blood pressure one reason for this is the presence of naturally occurring nitrates in beets which increase nitric oxide in the blood vessels allowing more oxygen to flow to your brain heart and muscles the anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger can also help manage blood pressure one study review found that ginger can lower blood pressure and decrease blood fat levels both of which help protect against heart disease two it gives you glowing skin if you're healthy from within it reflects on the outside beetroot and ginger juice act as a great blood purifier which is key in keeping your skin glowing and healthy meat roots are also rich in vitamin c which helps in clearing blemishes and evens out your skin tone while giving in a natural glow three it's a great way to detox naturally beet roots are cleanser and helps to flush out toxins ginger has also proved an effective detoxing food with some studies linking it to benefits and weight loss and there's definitely some promising research linking ginger to weight management 4. boost energy and stamina beetroot and ginger juice helps to open the blood vessels and increase the flow of oxygen throughout your body making you feel more energetic and active that's why so many people prefer to drink this juice in the morning so they can enjoy an energized start to the day it has often been promoted as a great workout drink and good for athletes thanks to its ability to increase oxygen to the muscles preparing them for the stress ahead 5. good for digestion beetroot and ginger juice is full of fiber which helps to regulate digestive processes and relieve constipation beetroot is also beneficial for good digestive health it's thought that beetroot increases stomach acid levels a glass of beetroot and ginger juice is also a great home remedy for an upset stomach research has linked multiple digestive benefits to ginger specifically feelings of nausea stomach upset and vomiting it may also help move food from the stomach to the small intestine for digestion and absorption 6. lowers blood glucose diabetes is a huge problem around the world and while drinking beetroot and ginger juice is far from a diabetes cure it may help someone in preventing and managing it research has linked some active compounds in ginger with improvements in insulin and metabolism and though high in sugar beetroot actually helps in regulating your blood sugar levels studies have shown that it doesn't lead to high glucose concentration in the blood the natural sugars in beet root are released very slowly in the body preventing sudden spikes 7. it's an anti-inflammatory like other produce ginger and beets contain antioxidant-like compounds that may reduce cell damage the root of ginger can also prevent inflammation by reducing cell signaling activity another reason to drink the two together eight can curb morning sickness pregnant women take note ginger and beetroot juice may help reduce symptoms of morning sickness research has found that it's safe to take ginger during pregnancy and it has shown improvement in morning sickness symptoms when compared to a placebo nine may lower your risk of cancer the cell protecting properties of ginger can lower the long-term risk of certain cancers that's because the spice and other flavorings can reduce cellular activity that causes dna changes cell death and proliferation of cancer cells so how can you make beetroot and ginger juice well it's quick and easy though you will need a juicer or a blender for the job start by washing and scrubbing your beets and ginger peel and cut the beets and ginger into small pieces that you can feed into the juicer many people also choose to squeeze a little lemon juice but that's completely up to you stir well and drink up if you only have a blender don't panic you can still make the juice with it just remove the peels from your ingredients blend until smooth and then strain through a nut milk bag or fine mesh sieve squeezing to get out as much juice as possible if you don't currently start your day with beetroot and ginger juice what are you waiting for this simple concoction is incredibly helpful so it's definitely worth adding to your diet sooner rather than later i hope you've enjoyed this video if you have please subscribe to this youtube channel and don't forget to give this video the thumbs up and i'm sure you're going to enjoy this next video on six health benefits of baking soda and apple cider vinegar tonic drink thanks for watching and bye for now you

videos.title: Top 3 Health Benefits of Consuming Turmeric | Healthy Tips | Boost Immunity with Turmeric

transcript.fullText:
Namaskar
Turmeric a golden spice, is used for its powerful medicinal
property since generation, this spice has being used since 3,000
years during the Vedic times, our ancestors discovered
numerous benefits of this Golden spice. So, the ancestors has passed that great knowledge
of turmeric, it is also used in our Indian rituals and ceremonies,
this excellent spice has many-many health benefits. Today I’m going to share many health benefits
of this excellent spice and how this spice is used to benefit
us, also you will learn how to incorporate HALDI in different ways. It’s main active component CURCUMIN, it
is responsible for many of the health benefits. 1. RELIEVES PAIN AND INFLAMATION
number one – it relieves pain and inflammation - Turmeric has
been widely used as a home remedy to treat pain, curcuminhas
anti-inflammatory properties, which helps relieves pain and
inflammation in our body it also release also relieves
osteoarthritis pain, and different joint pains, since it reduces
inflammation, it improves the joint mobility also. Also, when someone gets hurt or has some body
pain usually we give hot milk with haldi - turmeric and that
helps. Turmeric also has antibacterial and antiseptic
properties, applying turmeric to cuts and wounds speeds
up the healing process and stop the spread of infection. 2. IMPROVES MEMORY
improves memory - Curcumin medicinal quality is in the
prevention of neuro degeneration, therefore it is it important to
add turmeric in your daily meals like – daals and sabji , this will
improve children’s brain power and it will also prevent age
related memory loss. 3. HELPS FIGHT DEPRESSION
number three it helps fight depression – Curcumin has a
positive effect on memory and moods, it helps to regulate
neurotransmitters such as DOPAMINE and SEROTONIN these
hormones are responsible for generating happy feeling. 4. PREVENTS CANCER
It helps to prevent cancer - Chronic inflammation is one of the
causes for cancer, curcumin has an anti-cancer characteristic that
helps to regulate body inflammatory processes, every morning
take a glass of warm water or a glass of herbal tea and add pinch
of turmeric in that to gain the benefit of turmeric 5. PROTECTS AGAINST VIRUSES it protects against viruses - Turmeric helps
in development of immune system, its anti-viral properties,
helps fighting against all the viruses, drink a glass of warm turmeric
milk at night, also to boost the immune system. have certain spices daily - take 1 teaspoon
full of Honey in that add a pinch of turmeric, pinch of black pepper,
add grated ginger or powder of ginger that is sooth,
mix it and have it any time in the day 6. GET GLOWING SKIN
six get glowing skip - Turmeric close down the oxidation
process and thereby close down premature ageing because of
turmeric antiseptic property it prevents breakouts and acne,
which helps to get clear and healthy skin. it also works wonders in removing dark spots
and dark circle to remove dark circle take a milk and pinch of
turmeric apply underneath eyes keep it for 10 minutes and
wash to remove dark spots and bring out its natural
flow, take 2 spoons of curds and pinch of turmeric mix
it well, and apply it on the face, once it dries off , after 15
minutes wash it with warm water. Even though turmeric is safe spice, you should
use it in moderation, also you should be aware of the
side-effects, so that you can prevent other health issues. Turmeric can cause mild digestive issues such
as – bloating, acid reflux, flatulence and diarrhea. Also, turmeric has blood thinning property,
so people were taking blood thinning medicine, should avoid. Turmeric is a great spice, that will help
improve quality of life, Turmeric has several therapeutic properties
that can be used to treat or prevent several ailments. So, specially in this pandemic time remember
to use the turmeric at least in the morning and at night
- in the morning with warm water, at night with warm milk and
help yourself, also use this excellent spice in your face
pack and help yourself to see that you get a beautiful glowing skin
you feel confident you feel energetic and enthusiastic and you
can then work with positivity, go ahead and use this spice nicely,
systematically and help yourself NAMASKAR
